reviewer_protocol.step_1: Read each query.prompt; decide what skill SHOULD have top-1.
reviewer_protocol.step_4: Set human_reviewer_override.expected_skill (existing-skill name or hypothetical-skill name with leading `~`).
reviewer_protocol.step_5: For queries tagged deferred_skill_probe, decide whether the deferred skill would actually fill the gap or whether an existing skill suffices.
reviewer_protocol.step_7: Aggregate: compute agreement-rate, deferred-skill-needed-rate, and the migration-justification metrics in docs/plans/skill-taxonomy-v5-and-gap-fill.md § Verification gate item 9.

queries.prompt: Whats the right empty state for a search results page with no matches
queries.authoring_note: Empty-state design is a recurring UX micro-pattern; tests whether the library has a coherent home for it.

agent_assessment.note: Empty-state design is a recurring UX micro-pattern; tests whether the library has a coherent home for it.

queries.prompt: User keeps getting confused about how to undo their last action — what UX patterns help
queries.authoring_note: Undo affordance is a Norman-style mental-model query; partial probe for mental-models gap.

agent_assessment.note: Undo affordance is a Norman-style mental-model query; partial probe for mental-models gap.

queries.prompt: Should I use a modal or a side panel for editing this item
queries.authoring_note: Modal-vs-panel is a classic UX decision; tests whether interaction-patterns owns it cleanly.

agent_assessment.note: Modal-vs-panel is a classic UX decision; tests whether interaction-patterns owns it cleanly.

queries.prompt: How do I think about progressive disclosure when designing a complex form
queries.authoring_note: Progressive disclosure is a named UX mental model (Nielsen); probes mental-models gap.

agent_assessment.note: Progressive disclosure is a named UX mental model (Nielsen); probes mental-models gap.

queries.prompt: I need to design a data table with sortable columns and selectable rows
queries.authoring_note: Data-table is a high-frequency UI pattern; tests composition coverage.

agent_assessment.note: Data-table is a high-frequency UI pattern; tests composition coverage.

queries.prompt: Whats the right button hierarchy when I have three actions of similar importance
queries.authoring_note: Button-hierarchy is a visual-design + UI decision; tests visual-hierarchy fit.

agent_assessment.note: Button-hierarchy is a visual-design + UI decision; tests visual-hierarchy fit.

queries.prompt: How should I show 50 plus items in a list — pagination, infinite scroll, or virtualization
queries.authoring_note: List rendering strategy spans UX + frontend perf; boundary query.

agent_assessment.note: List rendering strategy spans UX + frontend perf; boundary query.

queries.prompt: Tooltip vs popover — which one for an inline help icon
queries.authoring_note: Component-vocab decision; tests fine-grained UI coverage.

agent_assessment.note: Component-vocab decision; tests fine-grained UI coverage.

queries.prompt: How do I pick a color palette that works in both light and dark mode
queries.authoring_note: Direct color-system query; tests router on existing color-system-design.

agent_assessment.note: Direct color-system query; tests router on existing color-system-design.

queries.prompt: Whats the right type scale for a dense data dashboard
queries.authoring_note: Type-scale query; tests typography-system fit.

agent_assessment.note: Type-scale query; tests typography-system fit.

queries.prompt: My icons look inconsistent across the app — how do I systematize them
queries.authoring_note: Icon-system is a sub-discipline of design-system-architecture; coverage probe.

agent_assessment.note: Icon-system is a sub-discipline of design-system-architecture; coverage probe.

queries.prompt: How do I balance whitespace without making the page look empty
queries.authoring_note: Whitespace is a Tufte-style visual-design concept; tests layout-composition.

agent_assessment.note: Whitespace is a Tufte-style visual-design concept; tests layout-composition.

queries.prompt: Im building a design system from scratch — what tokens do I need first
queries.authoring_note: Design-token entry-point query; tests whether existing skill answers it.

agent_assessment.note: Design-token entry-point query; tests whether existing skill answers it.

queries.prompt: How do I structure components so they compose well across products
queries.authoring_note: Direct probe for component-architecture deferred skill.

agent_assessment.note: Direct probe for component-architecture deferred skill.

queries.prompt: Whats the difference between primitives, patterns, and templates in a design system
queries.authoring_note: Brad Frost atomic-design vocabulary; secondary probe for component-architecture.

agent_assessment.note: Brad Frost atomic-design vocabulary; secondary probe for component-architecture.

queries.prompt: Where should I do form validation — client, server, or both
queries.authoring_note: Cross-boundary dev question; tests whether form-ux-architecture or client-server-boundary surfaces.

agent_assessment.note: Cross-boundary dev question; tests whether form-ux-architecture or client-server-boundary surfaces.

queries.prompt: I have a list of items that needs filtering, sorting, and pagination — whats the architecture
queries.authoring_note: Probe for state-management deferred skill; list-state is the canonical use case.

agent_assessment.note: Probe for state-management deferred skill; list-state is the canonical use case.

queries.prompt: How do I structure a Next.js app for the App Router
queries.authoring_note: Next App Router query; tests rendering-models / frontend-architecture coverage.

agent_assessment.note: Next App Router query; tests rendering-models / frontend-architecture coverage.

queries.prompt: Should this be a Server Component or a Client Component
queries.authoring_note: Wave 2 skill (client-server-boundary) was authored explicitly for this query.

queries.prompt: How do I handle state that needs to live across multiple routes
queries.authoring_note: Second probe for state-management; cross-route state is the harder case.

agent_assessment.note: Second probe for state-management; cross-route state is the harder case.

queries.prompt: How do I write tests for code that calls an external API
queries.authoring_note: Wave 4 skill (test-doubles-design) was authored for exactly this question.

queries.prompt: How do I make sure my refactor doesnt break anything
queries.authoring_note: Tests refactor + testing-strategy coverage.

queries.authoring_note: Wave 3 skill (streaming-architecture).
